published_prior_art.cite: WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, arXiv:2507.11780v1, Sections 2–3: the same softmax/debiased optimal-value route gives pointwise root-n inference, but not this finite-union uniform-honesty claim.
CaiLow2004, Proposition 1 and Sections 2.1–2.2: two-point expected-length and ordered-modulus lower-bound machinery for adaptive confidence intervals.
WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, Sections 2–3: softmax bias/remainder balancing already yields the corresponding root-n smoothing regime under a near-tie regularity condition.
CaiLow2004, Sections 2.2 and 5: finite-union ordered-modulus adaptation theory, restricted there to Gaussian linear-functionals.

novelty_flags.label: conj:uniform-honesty-upper-length / proposal kernel
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=field: the core reuses the published softmax orthogonal-score route and supplies neither a matched causal adaptation frontier, a strict rate/constant improvement over a named result, nor a first published-method bound; finite-union uniformization alone is not yet a demonstrated Stat frontier advance.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Constructs bias-aware honest nonparametric confidence intervals and derives bandwidth/efficiency results under predeclared function-class restrictions.
Develops expected-length adaptation theory for linear functionals, including ordered/between-class moduli, finite fixed unions, and two-point lower bounds.
Studies inference for the optimal-rule value, characterizes pathwise differentiability, and gives root-n confidence-interval methodology even at nonregular laws.
Develops subagging confidence intervals for optimal-value inference under nonregularity, proves nominal coverage, and compares average interval length with existing procedures.
Proposes model-free robust/resampling inference for optimal treatment regimes and asymptotically accurate inference for both regime parameters and the optimal value function.
Develops adaptive smoothing for the mean outcome under an optimal treatment regime and an asymptotic-variance lower bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class.

Introduces softmax smoothing plus a Neyman-orthogonal score for optimal-policy values, permits positive tie mass, and balances smoothing bias with nuisance error to obtain root-n inference under a near-zero gap density condition.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 112(520) (2017), pp. 1381–1392
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Studies rilpivirine versus efavirenz individualized regimes and uses G-computation, IPW, and augmented IPW with censoring/missing data handled under monotone coarsening.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the generic finite-union upper claim with a sharp causal adaptation theorem: identify explicit ordered pairs of the declared strata for which the testing modulus yields a nonzero penalty, and give a matching connected-interval construction up to constants or logarithmic factors.

State a strict comparison to WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025: prove uniform honesty under a precisely wider near-tie law class than its density condition, or a strictly weaker Hölder/nuisance threshold, and make that comparison a main statement with theorem locations cited.

Anchor every optimal-value comparator to its theorem/section and state whether the proposed score is algebraically the binary specialization of its softmax score; then retain only the genuinely unaddressed uniform/adaptation component.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a concrete sharp finite-union adaptation frontier or a strict, source-anchored extension of the published softmax result; otherwise pivot from the routine uniformization kernel.
